what_it_does: Records the analysis of the 'landing-mockups' source folder (3 tall fixed-width web pages, 1223px wide) as the web-surface stress test of the design model, concluding that the containment-tree/zoning/atom grammar transfers to a scrolling web surface with no new grammar, only web-chrome atoms.
observations: Header gives source provenance: ingest/landing-mockups/, 3 pages, dims 1223 x {4630,6967,7334}, fixed-width scrolling web surface, marked '4th folder'.
Verdict: the model transfers intact — a slide becomes a 'section band', the deck's vertical sequence becomes scroll, with the same Section/Zone/Group/Atom subtree inside each band.
Maps deck concepts to web equivalents: Slide->section band, Sequence->scroll, Zoning->alternating band backgrounds plus a dark footer band, Atoms transfer 1:1.

Lists genuinely-new web additions: persistent top nav + segmented audience toggle (Architects|Developers gold pills), hero band with down-chevron scroll cue, content-LOD placeholder/wireframe boxes, pricing comparison table, repeated CTA bands, dark footer.
Reads the grey placeholder boxes ('Guided tour here would be ideal') as direct evidence of the template-with-typed-slots model — the page authored at a structural LOD before assets land.
Confirms across 4 folders (marked four checkmarks): gold ramp, near-white zoning ladder, frame/footer signature, triangle bullets, thin icons, soft panels, narrative arc, negative space.

Extends AXES.md: surface axis now spans paged (slide) <-> scrolling (web); 'Slide' node generalises to a 'band/frame' that is paged or stacked; audience-variant unified as one {template + audience param} mechanism across surfaces.
Explicitly states 'No contradictions' — the web surface needed zero new grammar.
standouts: The reframe of scroll as just the Deck->Slide level re-rendered as stacked bands: motion=temporal traversal becomes scroll=spatial traversal of the same tree.
Audience-variant unified across three forms: variant slides, variant pages, and a live toggle — all the same {template + audience param}.
Treating the wireframe placeholder boxes as evidence of a typed-slot LOD model rather than as incomplete artwork.
conventions: evidence-diff discipline: marks each finding as confirms / extends (pencil) / new (plus) / contradicts (warning).
axis=f(content,position): surface is a generative axis (paged<->scrolling) not a separate design.
containment-tree as the invariant carried across surfaces.
single-mechanism reuse: one audience-variant mechanism spans slides, pages, and toggles.
concerns: Findings rest on 3 fixed-width (1223px) pages; the doc itself notes width-fixed responsive, so mobile-reflow claims are inferred from the deck rules rather than observed on a narrow web viewport.
Multiple cross-folder confidence claims ('Confirmed across 4 folders') are summary assertions whose underlying per-folder evidence lives in other analysis docs, not here.
notes: The folder analysis where the deck DNA is proven to survive a scrolling web page intact — slide becomes band, sequence becomes scroll, only web-chrome atoms are new.
questions: How do the fixed-width 1223px pages actually reflow on a narrow mobile viewport — is that verified anywhere, or only predicted from the deck's responsive rules?
Is the 'content-LOD placeholder box' an intentional authored state in the source, or the analyst's interpretation of unfinished mockups?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — it is framed as the web-surface stress test and it executes that test, mapping each deck primitive to its web form and logging only additive web-chrome atoms.
intention_signals: Inferred: the analysis is trying to prove the generative model is surface-agnostic — that a web landing page is not a different design language but the same containment tree traversed by scroll — so that the system can emit web and decks from one source, and to log only the truly new web-chrome atoms rather than reinvent grammar.
novelty: Frames a scrolling web page as a re-rendering of a paged deck's top level (slide->band, page->scroll) and treats wireframe placeholders as proof of a typed-slot LOD model — an unusual structural reading of marketing mockups.
contribution: Contributes the surface-axis extension (paged<->scrolling, Slide generalised to band/frame), the unification of audience-variant across slides/pages/toggle, and a logged set of web-chrome atoms (persistent nav, segmented toggle, comparison-pricing table, repeated CTA band, scroll-cue, content-LOD placeholder) that a fused system would keep.
summary_for_embedding: Analysis of three tall fixed-width web landing mockups as the web-surface stress test, finding the deck's containment tree, zoning ladder, gold ramp, atoms and narrative arc transfer to a scrolling page (slide->band, sequence->scroll) with only additive web-chrome atoms.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to prove the generative design model is surface-agnostic by mapping a scrolling web page onto the same containment tree as a paged deck, extending the surface axis to span paged and scrolling and unifying audience-variant across slides, pages and live toggles.
